title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on an education website:

'We want to hear about online learning! Write a review of an educational website, app, or online course you have used. Tell us what it covers, how effective it is, and whether you would recommend it to other learners.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Khan Academy — A Truly Remarkable Free Learning Resource

As someone who has used Khan Academy extensively while preparing for mathematics and science exams, I can confidently say it is one of the most valuable free educational resources available online.

The platform covers an enormous range of subjects, from basic arithmetic to university-level calculus, as well as sciences, history, and economics. Every topic is explained through short, clearly structured video lessons followed by interactive practice exercises that adapt to your level. The immediate feedback on exercises is particularly helpful, as it allows you to identify and address gaps in understanding straight away.

What sets Khan Academy apart is its genuine accessibility — everything is completely free, and the content is carefully designed to suit learners of varying abilities and backgrounds. The progress-tracking dashboard also helps you stay motivated by visualising your improvement over time.

If there is a weakness, it is that the platform can feel somewhat impersonal compared to a real classroom, and some learners may struggle with self-motivation.

Nevertheless, I would wholeheartedly recommend Khan Academy to any student looking to consolidate or extend their knowledge independently.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine for students:

'Homework: helpful habit or unnecessary burden? Write an article giving your views on whether homework should be abolished in schools. Consider arguments on both sides before reaching your own conclusion.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Should Homework Be Abolished? The Debate Continues

Few topics in education generate as much controversy as homework. Students often dread it, parents frequently struggle to enforce it, and researchers cannot agree on whether it actually works. So should schools simply scrap it?

Arguments in favour of abolition are compelling. Excessive homework creates stress and reduces the time young people have for sleep, hobbies, and social activities — all of which are essential for healthy development. Furthermore, studies suggest homework has minimal impact on attainment at primary level and mixed results at secondary level.

However, the case for keeping homework is also strong. Regular independent practice reinforces classroom learning, develops self-discipline, and prepares students for the self-directed study required at university. The key, arguably, is quality over quantity.

My view is that homework should not be abolished, but radically reimagined. Shorter, more purposeful tasks — such as reading, creative projects, or revision — are far more valuable than repetitive exercises completed for the sake of it.

The goal should always be learning, not the appearance of learning.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You would like to suggest some improvements to your school's facilities. Write a letter to your headteacher outlining at least two specific improvements you think should be made, explaining why each one would benefit students, and suggesting how the school could fund these changes.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Ms Harrison,

I am writing to suggest some improvements to our school's facilities that I believe would significantly benefit students' learning and wellbeing.

Firstly, I would strongly recommend upgrading the school library. At present, the space is underused largely because the book collection is outdated and the study area has insufficient seating and poor lighting. A modernised library with comfortable study spaces, current reading materials, and reliable Wi-Fi would provide students with a much-needed quiet environment for independent study.

Secondly, the outdoor sports facilities are in urgent need of renovation. The main playing field becomes waterlogged in winter, making it unusable for months at a time. Resurfacing the area with all-weather materials would allow physical education lessons and after-school clubs to continue year-round, greatly benefiting students' health.

Regarding funding, I would suggest applying for the government's School Improvement Grant, as well as approaching local businesses for sponsorship in exchange for appropriate recognition.

I sincerely hope you will give these proposals serious consideration.

Yours sincerely,
A Student Representative
